mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated FEDFUNDS above 5% combined with CPIAUCSL inflation still running above the Fed's implicit target compresses equity valuations by keeping the discount rate high, producing negative or muted S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter until a policy pivot is signaled.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered high-rate/high-inflation instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE trend over the trailing 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumption pullback and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day SPX returns following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp are positive in over 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as a persistent always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS MoM growth and forward 5-day SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO over 3 consecutive months signals contracting industrial output and impending earnings weakness in cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 3 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered 3-month INDPRO decline instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST combined with a rising FEDFUNDS environment signals that tight monetary policy is choking housing-sector demand, an early indicator of broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle impacting rate-sensitive sectors
failure_condition: If joint HOUST decline and elevated FEDFUNDS triggers precede positive 20-day SPX returns in over 55% of cases historically, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Robust monthly PAYEMS gains above 200k signal continued labor market strength that supports consumer income and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the economy is not in a late-cycle overheating regime where strong jobs data raises rate-hike fears.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative or neutral monetary policy
failure_condition: If strong PAYEMS prints during periods of FEDFUNDS > 5% are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in over 55% of instances (indicating regime flip not captured), reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A declining trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 6 months signals an easing cycle that lowers the discount rate on future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on directional signal.
failure_condition: If the sign of the FEDFUNDS 6-month trend shows no statistically significant relationship (p > 0.10) with forward 5-day SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth decelerates below trend while INDPRO simultaneously contracts, the joint signal indicates a broad-based real economic slowdown that overwhelms sector-specific noise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1% and INDPRO YoY change < 0%
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered joint GDPC1/INDPRO slowdown instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.
